joke.raw_joke: I got thrown out of my local park after arranging the squirrels by height. — They didn't like me critter sizing

blocks.content:
### seed-188

**MILO_OFFICIAL_SCRIPT_v1**

seed_id: seed-188
edit_level: polish

script:
- setup: "I got thrown out of my local park for arranging the squirrels by height."
- punchline: "Turns out nobody likes critter sizing."
- tag: ~

changes_made: Changed "after arranging" to "for arranging" — tighter causal link. Added "Turns out" to open the punchline — it frames the line as a discovered universal truth, which is funnier than a personal grievance. Changed "didn't like me critter sizing" to "nobody likes critter sizing" — the generalization is better; this is apparently a park-wide policy, maybe a broader social consensus, and the narrator is just finding out.

caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "I got thrown out of my local park for arranging the squirrels by height." Punchline: "Turns out nobody likes critter sizing." — "critter sizing" should ideally be bolded or highlighted on the punchline card to give the viewer a visual beat to parse "criticizing" underneath it.

timing_notes: Setup delivered with mild aggrievement — he was ejected for something that seemed reasonable. 0.4s pause. Punchline at normal pace with slight emphasis on "critter" — it needs to hit clearly enough that the listener catches "criticizing" a half-second after landing. Do not rush the final word. Hold it just slightly.

tts_notes: "Critter sizing" — KRIT-er SY-zing. The second syllable of "sizing" needs stress: SY, not siz. If the syllable stress is wrong the wordplay gets muddy. Phonetic fallback: "KRIT-er SY-zing."

quality_flag: solid

---

### seed-188

**RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1**

**Primary Cast:**

| Role | Cast | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Narrator (ejected from park, squirrel height incident) | Milo Quip — Snarky Student persona (adapted: Teen Who Did This and Sees No Problem With It) | The joke requires a narrator who arranged squirrels by height and will describe the ejection as the unreasonable part of the story. Milo's "youth skeptic / punchline sniper" archetype delivers the absurdist premise with complete deadpan sincerity — he would do this, it made sense at the time, and he is mildly aggrieved to have been ejected for it. The indignation is real. He stands by the activity. |

**Adaptation Notes:**
- Milo: Casual teen wardrobe. Post-ejection energy — slightly disheveled, maybe a leaf on his jacket. Could be sitting just outside the park gate or on a bench nearby. No props required, but a park-adjacent setting (bench, grass, fence line) establishes the scene immediately.
- Visual background option for setup shot: 2-3 squirrels arranged in ascending height visible in background illustration — small, medium, large, spaced evenly. They should look deliberate. The arrangement is tidy. This is the work.

**Shot Intent:**
- **Setup:** Milo to camera, describing the incident with mild affront. His body language is still slightly in the "arranging" posture — arms at different heights, maybe. He was doing something organized. He was interrupted.
- **Punchline:** Hold on Milo. "Turns out nobody likes critter sizing." He delivers this as a universal law of nature he has only just discovered. His expression does not change after the punchline. He still doesn't see the problem. He might do it again.
